title: PokéAPI Resource Identity, URL Construction, Pagination, and Fair Use · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs pokéapi resource identity, url construction, pagination, and fair use from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for fixed-origin PokéAPI resource and fair-use policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.
Distinguish resource type, numeric ID, canonical lowercase name, list query, and response self URL without unsafe equivalence.
Construct path segments and limit or offset queries with net/url while rejecting slash, control, and oversized input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Pokedex Go Fixed Origin Policy · changed case · Review Pokedex G…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 7258 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit fixed-origin resource URLs, bounded pagination, and fair-use request policy; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.
